scenario_title: Mixed add and delete of the same RRset in one UPDATE
test_rationale: This test isolates the underspecified intra-message conflict resolution rule by sending one syntactically valid UPDATE that both adds and deletes the same A RRset at www.campus.edu. in a single packet. Because RFC 2136 does not define whether the server must treat the update section as ordered, set-like, or contradictory, compliant servers may either accept and remove the RRset, reject the packet, or preserve the original data. The follow-up QUERY makes the resulting RRset state directly observable.

group_1.behavior: Processes the UPDATE sequentially and accepts it; the later delete_rrset removes the A RRset, leaving no A answer for www.campus.edu.

group_2.behavior: Rejects the internally contradictory UPDATE as a server error or data-state conflict, leaving the preexisting A RRset unchanged.

expected_divergence.why_divergence: RFC 2136 defines add_rr and delete_rrset as separate update actions, but does not specify how a single UPDATE message must resolve contradictory edits to the same owner/type, nor whether update-section operations are applied in order or normalized before execution. As a result, implementations may either apply the edits sequentially or reject the packet, producing divergent reply codes and/or final RRset contents.
